steps.title: Find the central claim
steps.content: The passage explains that The Yellow Admiral's abrupt ending is only a problem if the novels are treated as separate independent texts. It argues that the Aubrey/Maturin books are better understood as one very long connected work.

steps.title: Rule out overstatements
steps.content: A adds unsupported claims about critical prestige. C says the novels have structural flaws, but the passage argues the abrupt endings make sense when the series is read properly. D claims two novels are especially complex, which the passage never says. The main idea is about how understanding the series' structure helps readers appreciate it. The answer is B.
